players.name: Thibaut Courtois
players.bio: The giant between the sticks — literally. At 6'6", Courtois is one of the world's finest keepers, winning the Golden Glove at Russia 2018. Champions League winner with Real Madrid, he's Belgium's last line of defence at his third World Cup.
players.facts: Courtois was named best keeper at the 2018 World Cup despite Belgium finishing 3rd — not bad for the runner-up prize.

players.name: Zeno Debast
players.bio: The new sheriff in town. Debast has rapidly grown into Belgium's defensive cornerstone at just 22, combining composure beyond his years with commanding aerial ability. A regular for Sporting CP, he's one of the Red Devils' brightest long-term investments.
players.facts: Debast was born in 2003 — the same year Belgium reached the World Cup quarter-finals. He's come a long way since nappies.

players.name: Arthur Theate
players.bio: A left-footed centre-back with pace and ambition, Theate has become a reliable figure in Belgium's backline. Having impressed in Ligue 1 and now the Bundesliga with Eintracht Frankfurt, he brings versatility and drive from the back.
players.facts: Theate was born in Liège, the same city as Axel Witsel — making them the most Liégeois defensive pairing in Belgian history.

players.name: Brandon Mechele
players.bio: A Club Brugge legend who's spent almost his entire career at the Jan Breydel Stadium. Mechele is a no-nonsense, experienced centre-back whose international opportunities have been limited but whose reliability is unquestioned.
players.facts: Mechele has made over 300 appearances for Club Brugge. He's practically part of the stadium furniture at this point.

players.name: Maxim De Cuyper
players.bio: An attack-minded left-back with a knack for goals, De Cuyper has brought energy and creativity to the Belgian flank. With four international goals from just 19 caps, the Brighton man is a genuine attacking threat from deep.
players.facts: De Cuyper has more international goals than some strikers — four in 19 caps is a return most left-backs can only dream of.

players.name: Axel Witsel
players.bio: The evergreen engine of Belgian football. At 37, Witsel is somehow still going — now at Girona — having served Belgium faithfully through three World Cups. A veteran of the Golden Generation, his reading of the game compensates for what age may have taken.
players.facts: Witsel once played in the Chinese Super League for Tianjin. From Liège to Lisbon to Liga — the man gets around.

players.name: Kevin De Bruyne
players.bio: Arguably the greatest midfielder of his generation. De Bruyne's passing range, vision, and shooting make him Belgium's talisman. Now at Napoli after his Manchester City era, KDB arrives at his third World Cup determined to finally win the big one.
players.kit: Nike Phantom GT Elite Dynamic Fit FG
players.facts: KDB has more Premier League assists than any player in a single season (2019-20, 20). He broke Thierry Henry's record.

players.name: Youri Tielemans
players.bio: Silky, intelligent, and criminally underrated. Tielemans is a box-to-box midfielder who scored that FA Cup final winner for Leicester in 2021. At Aston Villa, he's found his stride again and is a key creative cog for Belgium.
players.facts: Tielemans scored the FA Cup-winning goal for Leicester in 2021 with a thunderbolt from 25 yards. Pure cinema.

players.name: Romelu Lukaku
players.bio: Belgium's all-time top scorer and a physical force of nature. Lukaku's 90 international goals speak volumes. The Napoli striker has had a turbulent club career but remains deadly in a red shirt, and arrives hungry for World Cup glory.
players.facts: Lukaku's 90 Belgium goals make him one of the top international scorers in European football history. He means business.

players.name: Leandro Trossard
players.bio: Reliable, versatile, and technically excellent, Trossard has thrived at Arsenal as a key squad player capable of playing across the front line. His composure in front of goal and work rate off the ball make him a perfect modern winger.
players.facts: Trossard scored a hat-trick for Brighton vs Liverpool in 2022 — then the game finished 3-3. He wasn't even annoyed.

players.name: Jérémy Doku
players.bio: Pure electricity on the wing. Doku is one of the most thrilling dribblers in world football — defenders simply cannot stop him when he's running at pace. The Manchester City flyer arrives at his first World Cup with something to prove.
players.facts: Doku completed more dribbles per 90 than almost any player in the Premier League in 2023-24. Defenders need shin guards just watching him.

players.name: Senne Lammens
players.bio: A young shot-stopper making his way in the game, Lammens joined Manchester United as a promising backup option. With only two caps to his name, this World Cup squad inclusion is a major stepping stone in the Zottegem-born keeper's development.
players.facts: Lammens is one of the few players at this World Cup who plays for Man United — a rare thing in 2026, some might say.

players.name: Mike Penders
players.bio: The youngest goalkeeper in the squad and yet to earn a senior cap, Penders is here to learn from the best. The Strasbourg stopper is highly rated for his age and is considered a future first-choice keeper for the Red Devils.
players.facts: Penders was born in 2005 — making him one of the youngest players at the entire 2026 World Cup tournament.

players.name: Dodi Lukébakio
players.bio: Explosive, direct, and with a habit for big-game goals, Lukébakio has been one of Benfica's key attackers. The Brussels-born winger possesses searing pace and a powerful shot that can unlock any defence on his day.
players.facts: Lukébakio once scored a hat-trick for Fortuna Düsseldorf against Bayern Munich in 2018. Yes, really. Bayern lost 3-3.

players.name: Thomas Meunier
players.bio: A marauding right-back with goals in his boots, Meunier has been a Belgium regular for over a decade. Now at Lille, the Bastogne-born veteran brings experience, physicality, and a surprisingly productive attacking record from full-back.
players.facts: Meunier has 10 goals for Belgium as a right-back. He scores more internationally than many strikers. Bit awkward for them.

players.name: Koni De Winter
players.bio: A composed and cultured centre-back who has earned a spot at AC Milan, De Winter is building a reputation in Serie A. Still only 23, he brings defensive intelligence and excellent distribution — a genuine prospect for Belgium's future.
players.facts: De Winter shares a birthday month with Romelu Lukaku — both born in June from Antwerp. Antwerp really is producing the goods.

players.name: Charles De Ketelaere
players.bio: After a difficult stint at AC Milan, De Ketelaere absolutely flourished at Atalanta — winning the Europa League in 2024. The Bruges-born playmaker's flair, technique, and vision make him one of Belgium's most exciting creative talents.
players.facts: CDK went on loan to Atalanta after flopping at Milan, then helped them win the Europa League. Best glow-up in football?

players.name: Joaquin Seys
players.bio: A young left-back from Belgium's coastal town of Ostend, Seys has developed well at Club Brugge and earned early international recognition. Still only 21, he's here to gain tournament experience alongside Belgium's more established names.
players.facts: Seys is from Ostend, a Belgian seaside resort. Whether he prefers sea views or silverware, this WC is the bigger wave.

players.name: Diego Moreira
players.full_name: Diego Manuel Jadon da Silva Moreira
players.bio: A Portuguese-heritage wide midfielder born in Liège, Moreira brings flair and creativity to Belgium's options. Still just 21 with only three caps, the Strasbourg winger is an exciting wildcard who could emerge as a key player.
players.facts: Moreira is of Portuguese descent playing for Belgium — yet another reason Franco-Belgian dinners get complicated.

players.name: Hans Vanaken
players.bio: A domestic giant who dominates the Belgian Pro League, Vanaken has been Club Brugge's heartbeat for years, winning multiple Golden Shoes. Often underused internationally, the creative midfielder is Belgium's most decorated domestic player.
players.facts: Vanaken has won the Belgian Golden Shoe — awarded to the league's best player — a record five times. Five. Times.

players.name: Timothy Castagne
players.bio: Versatile, energetic, and technically sound, Castagne has been a dependable option at right-back or wing-back for Belgium across two World Cups. Now at Fulham, the Arlon native brings experience and reliability to the defensive line.
players.facts: Castagne scored twice at Euro 2020 in Belgium's opening group game — then got injured and missed the rest. Timing is everything.

players.name: Alexis Saelemaekers
players.bio: A dynamic and hard-working wide midfielder who can play on either flank, Saelemaekers has been a useful squad player at AC Milan. His pressing intensity and work rate make him a manager's dream as an impact substitute.
players.facts: Saelemaekers spent a loan spell at Bologna in 2023-24, helping them qualify for the Champions League. Not bad for a cameo.

players.name: Nicolas Raskin
players.bio: A combative defensive midfielder with a nose for goal and a nasty tackle to boot, Raskin has impressed at Rangers after developing in Belgium. The Liège-born battler adds steel and energy to Belgium's midfield options.
players.facts: Raskin plays for Rangers in Glasgow — he's probably the only Belgian regularly hearing bagpipes on a matchday. Character builder.

players.name: Amadou Onana
players.full_name: Amadou Ba Zeund Georges Mvom Onana
players.bio: Born in Dakar but raised in Belgium, Onana is a physical phenomenon in midfield — towering, powerful, and increasingly refined technically. The Aston Villa powerhouse is Belgium's midfield enforcer and one of Europe's most exciting young players.
players.facts: Onana stands 6'3" and runs like a winger. He's basically what happens if you put a No.6 and a No.10 in a blender.

players.name: Nathan Ngoy
players.bio: A promising young centre-back who has developed at Lille, Ngoy is still building his international experience with four caps. The Brussels-born defender has the physical and technical attributes to develop into a key Belgium player.
players.facts: Ngoy made his Belgium debut as a teenager — he's barely old enough to remember the Golden Generation's peak years.

players.name: Matias Fernandez-Pardo
players.bio: One of the youngest players in Belgium's squad, Fernandez-Pardo is a forward with pace and an eye for goal. Born in Brussels in 2005, he's still uncapped at senior level but was included as one of Belgium's most exciting young strikers.
players.facts: Fernandez-Pardo was born in 2005 — the year Belgium were ranked 66th in the world. He's arrived with the renaissance.
